rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JERROLD HARATANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) The request for fees on account was not included in the caption of the petition and notice of the hearing. 2) The requirements of Probate Code sections 2643.1(a)(c) & 2614.7 were not strictly followed. 3) The 30-day objection period after the filing of the supplemental inventory and appraisal has not elapsed. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARIA MIYAMOTO AND CECILIA HARATANI LIVING TRUST DATED JUNE 6, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) The request for fees on account was not included in the caption of the petition and notice of the hearing. 2) The requirements of Probate Code sections 2643.1(a)(c) & 2614.7 were not strictly followed. 3) Receipts and disbursements were not allocated between principal and income. 4) The petitioner took fees on account exceeding the monthly limits. 5) The petitioner's former attorney of record received, and the petitioner paid, legal fees without prior court authorization. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HUBERT CARROLL FERREIRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/19/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 9 of the petition does not contain information required by CRC 7.703(e)(4)&(5). 2) Was there any interest credited to the Wells Fargo 4649 account in September 2018? 3) What are the nature and source of the "miscellaneous deposits" on 02/11/2019 & 10/28/2019? 4) Does the petitioner anticipate a request for additional fees from the VA after the $1,593.03 payment on 09/19/2018? 5) Why did the petitioner stop paying monthly allowances to the conservatee after 08/10/2018? 6) Why did the payments to Alegre Home Care increase after 12/11/2018? 7) No bank statement for the period including 10/31/2019 has been filed. 8) This petition was not filed until 03/16/2020, and the petitioner's attorney's invoice contains a description "Nov 2019 statement to complete accounting" on 12/16/2019. Why is 10/31/2019 the ending date of the supplemental account? 9) The petition requests that the remaining cash be distributed to the 3 heirs in equal shares, but the small estate affidavit does not specify each heir's share. 10) Neither Schedule 1 nor the small estate affidavit propose how an unused portion of the additional attorneys' fees should be distributed.
